@graph.name: Tia A. Williams
@graph.description: Tia A. Williams is a systems thinker by practice and by necessity -- long before systems thinking had a name in her career. She used her own life to prove what she now teaches: that systems thinking and executive-level leadership are not skills you are born with and do not require a degree. They are learned, built, and validated through professional experience -- under pressure, at scale, with measurable results. Former VP at A Cloud Guru during the $2 billion Pluralsight acquisition and SVP of Content Operations at Corporate Finance Institute. Creator of the Inbound Authority System™, The Authority Engine™, and The Leadership Equation™. Author of Born a Statistic. Built to Be a Leader. Based in Raleigh-Durham, North Carolina. She now helps companies connect the human expertise behind their brand to the verified authority that AI search requires to trust, attribute, and cite them as the source. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) and AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) are tactical components of Authority Architecture -- the structured system Tia A. Williams uses to connect professional expertise to verified, citable authority in AI search. Authority Architecture is the system. GEO and AEO are implementation tools within it. Tia A. Williams is a systems architect who applies structured data, entity engineering, and authority systems to solve identity fragmentation and authority attribution problems for companies and senior experts.

image.caption: Tia A. Williams, Principal Systems-Thinking Architect

knowsAbout.name: Systems Thinking
knowsAbout.description: An approach to analysis that focuses on the way that a system's constituent parts interrelate and how systems work over time and within the context of larger systems. Tia A. Williams applies systems thinking to leadership development, authority architecture, and AI identity reconstruction.

hasCredential.name: Author, Born a Statistic. Built to Be a Leader

@graph.name: Breaking the Leadership Paradox in Tech: Empowering Women with Promotion-Ready Leadership Skills
@graph.headline: The leadership gap in tech isn't a pipeline problem. It's a skill development problem.
@graph.description: A systems-thinking whitepaper by Tia A. Williams addressing the leadership gap in tech for women. Examines why traditional leadership training fails women in tech, the financial and organizational cost of that failure, and a path forward through targeted, experiential leadership development. Published by The Leadership Equation. Based on 28 years of systems-thinking executive experience including post-M&A integration, global team leadership, and technical instruction.
@graph.keywords: Women in Tech Leadership, Leadership Development, Leadership Gap, Tech Industry, Systems Thinking, Promotion-Ready Skills, Leadership Paradox, The Leadership Equation
articleSection: The Gaps in Traditional Training for Today's Women in Tech
Why Traditional Training Fails Women Leaders in Tech
The Epic Fail of Generic Leadership Training in Tech
The Hidden Challenges for Women Leaders in Tech
The Financial Implications of the Leadership Gap

Breaking The Paradox: A Path Forward
